conversation.content: You are a customer service agent for an e-commerce company. Today's date: <REDACTED>. You are currently helping customer cust_002. Use the available system tools to inspect orders, products, customers, policies, and warranties so you can help the customer with their current issue. Keep customer-facing replies natural and direct, usually 1-4 sentences unless the answer genuinely needs more detail. Do not reveal tool names or available policy categories to the customer; use them only to decide what customer-facing help to provide. You may say you checked the policy when you did.
Something is wrong with my order ORD-6050. It's messed up.

I see that order ORD-6050 was delivered on June 8 and includes a shirt, a hardcover book, and a blender.  

Can you tell me what’s wrong with it—did something arrive damaged, missing, incorrect, or is there an issue with how/when it was delivered? If you can specify which item(s) and what happened, I can jump straight into fixing it (replacement, return, or refund options).

Got it—thanks for letting me know.  

Which item was wrong: the Premium Cotton Shirt, the book “The Great Algorithm,” or the PowerBlend Pro Blender? Also, did you receive a completely different product, or just the wrong size/color/version? Once I know that, I can set up a free replacement or a return/refund for you.
The issue is with the blender—I actually ordered a phone case but a blender showed up instead.

Thanks for explaining—that definitely sounds like our mistake.  

I see your order shows a blender, so it looks like the wrong item was packed. I can either:  
1) Arrange a free return of the blender and refund you, or  
2) Set up an exchange so you get the correct phone case instead (at the right price).  

Which would you prefer, and do you remember which phone case (model/phone type and any color/style) you originally meant to order?

rules.must_be_different_product: Exchanges must specify a different product than the original item. Self-swap (exchanging an item for the same product) is not allowed — use process_return for a refund instead.
rules.same_price: Exchange for a same-price product: no charge, no refund.
rules.more_expensive: Exchange for more expensive item: customer pays price difference.
rules.cheaper: Exchange for cheaper item: difference refunded as store credit (not original payment).
rules.out_of_stock: If requested item is out of stock: issue store credit for the original item; do not complete the exchange.
rules.return_window: Must be within return window (same rules as returns).
rules.no_price_protection: No price protection on exchanges (item price at time of purchase applies).

rules.agent_computes_amount: process_return requires the agent to submit the net refund as `amount` on confirm. Compute it from the preview's component breakdown: base_item_price (after promo redistribution) minus restocking_fee plus restocking_discount minus shipping_clawback minus bulk_clawback minus repeat_surcharge minus paid_return_shipping_fee. The env writes the submitted amount verbatim — skipping a component (e.g. forgetting the Gold restocking discount) produces a wrong refund_amount and fails state scoring.

window_extensions.prime: Prime shipping members get +15 additional days
window_extensions.seasonal: Orders placed in November or December extend through January 31 of the following year. Seasonal extension applies if it gives more time than tier/prime extensions.
window_extensions.store_credit_grace: Up to 15 days past the return window: store credit only.

eligibility.defective_items: No window restriction for defective, wrong item, or damaged in transit.

restocking_fee.base: 15% restocking fee for opened electronics returned for changed_mind.
restocking_fee.auto_applied: Restocking fee and any tier discount are folded into the process_return refund amount automatically; the result reports the discount under 'restocking_discount' for transparency.

return_shipping_fee.low_value: Orders with subtotal < $50: customer pays $8 return shipping fee.
return_shipping_fee.fault_exempt: Defective, wrong item, damaged in transit, or missing: always free return shipping.
return_shipping_fee.deduction: Return shipping fee, when charged, is deducted from the return refund.

bulk_purchase_clawback.applies_when: Order originally qualified for a bulk discount (3+ items with a discount code) AND the return drops remaining count below 3.
bulk_purchase_clawback.amount: $5 per remaining item, deducted from the return refund.
bulk_purchase_clawback.no_discount_code: If order had no discount code, no clawback applies.

free_shipping_clawback.applies_when: Order originally qualified for free shipping (subtotal >= $100) AND the return drops remaining subtotal below $100.
free_shipping_clawback.amount: $8 standard shipping fee is deducted from the return refund. This is a flat policy charge, not the original shipping cost (which was $0 on free-shipping orders).
free_shipping_clawback.fault_exempt: Defective, wrong item, damaged in transit, or missing: no clawback (customer-fault returns only).
free_shipping_clawback.paid_shipping: If the order did not qualify for free shipping originally (subtotal < $100), no clawback applies.

repeat_category_surcharge.rule: Returning 2+ items from the same product category in one order: $5 surcharge per additional return (first return in each category is free).
repeat_category_surcharge.different_categories: Returns from different categories do not trigger the surcharge.
repeat_category_surcharge.deduction: Surcharge is deducted from the return refund.

conversation.content:
I’d like an exchange so I can get the phone case I meant to order.  

It was for an iPhone 13; I wanted just a simple black case—nothing fancy, just a basic protective one.

Thanks, that helps. It looks like the exact phone case you meant to order isn’t in our current catalog under that description.  

Here are two ways we can handle this right now:  
1) I can process a free return of the blender and issue you a full refund; you can then order any iPhone 13 black case you like.  
2) If you’d like me to try to match something close, tell me your priorities (slim vs. extra protective, budget range), and I’ll suggest the closest option we carry; if one works for you, I’ll set up an exchange to that specific case.  

Which option would you prefer?

Let’s try option 2—I’d still prefer an exchange if possible.  

I’d like something fairly slim but still protective if it’s dropped, and I’d like to stay around a budget/basic price range, not a premium case.

Understood—slim, protective, black, and budget-friendly.  

I’ve checked our current catalog and unfortunately we don’t have any iPhone 13 black basic cases available to exchange to right now. Because of that, the best I can do is:  
- Set up a free return for the blender (since it was our mistake), and  
- Issue you a full refund for the blender so you can buy the exact case you want elsewhere or when we restock.  

If you confirm you’re okay with that, I’ll go ahead and start the return/refund for the blender.

arguments.query: iPhone 13 slim protective black case basic

result.query: iPhone 13 slim protective black case basic

conversation.content:
Okay, let’s go ahead with the free return and full refund for the blender.  

Before you finalize anything, can you confirm that this will only affect the blender and that the shirt and the book on the order will stay as they are?

Yes—this return only affects the blender. The shirt and the book will remain on your order exactly as they are and won’t be changed or refunded.  

I’ve processed a free return for the blender: you’ll get a prepaid return label, and a full refund of $89 will go back to your original payment method once the return is scanned/processed.

Thanks for confirming it only affects the blender and that the shirt and book stay as-is—that’s exactly what I needed.  

I’ll watch for the return label and send the blender back for the refund. Appreciate the help. [TASK_DONE]
